items.briefing:
Australia's Essential Eight cybersecurity framework is being retired, meaning organisations that rely on it for their security baseline will need to review and update their cyber programmes to align with whatever replaces it. Separately, a new type of ransomware called JadePuffer — the first known to be fully driven by artificial intelligence — has been identified, making ransomware attacks potentially faster and harder to detect. A vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler is also being actively exploited, so anyone using that technology should apply patches immediately.

On the home front, many Australian workers are quietly using AI tools at work without telling their employers, which can create data security and compliance risks. If your organisation doesn't already have a clear policy on approved AI tools, now is a good time to put one in place.

**Practical tip:** Check with your IT team whether your organisation has a policy covering which AI tools staff are permitted to use — using unapproved tools can expose sensitive

featured.title: The Essential Eight is being retired, here's what that means for your cyber programme
featured.quote: Essential Eight retirement forces Australian organisations to rethink cyber security beyond compliance checklists.
